key_points: Biodiversity supports ecosystem services such as pollination and nutrient cycling
Loss of biodiversity disrupts ecological balance and food chains
Forest management policies regulate the use and protection of forest resources
Environmental movements highlight the role of people in conservation
Sustainable development balances economic growth with environmental protection
examples: Chipko Movement showed the power of grassroots environmental activism
Sacred groves preserve rare species due to cultural traditions
Project Tiger created protected habitats for endangered species
Joint Forest Management encourages sustainable use of forest resources
Community conservation in Rajasthan protects wildlife habitats
formulas: Sustainable development = Development without harming future resources
Biodiversity conservation = Protection of species + habitats + ecosystems
Environmental management = Government policies + community participation
exam_tips: Compare different forest categories in answers
Link biodiversity conservation with sustainable development
Use case studies like Chipko Movement in long answers
